highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.8, the latest addition to the Claude model family. This release is significant for Claude Code users as newer model versions typically bring improved reasoning, coding accuracy, and better instruction-following. Keep an eye on the Anthropic blog for details on how to select this model within your Claude Code setup.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.163: Version Enforcement & Plugin List Command
highlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.163 introduces managed settings `requiredMinimumVersion` and `requiredMaximumVersion`, allowing teams to enforce specific version ranges and prevent Claude Code from starting outside those bounds. A new `/plugin list` command (with `--enabled`/`--disabled` flags) makes it easy to audit which plugins are active in your environment. These are especially useful for teams managing shared VS Code setups across multiple developers.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.160: Safer Shell & Config File Writes
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.160 adds a confirmation prompt before Claude Code writes to shell startup files like `.zshenv` or `.bash_login`, protecting you from unintended command execution. Additionally, `acceptEdits` mode now pauses before modifying build-tool config files such as `.npmrc` that could grant code execution privileges. This is an important safety update—make sure you're running this version or later.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a concise, focused walkthrough of Claude Code's core features in just 30 minutes, making it ideal for developers who want to get productive quickly without a lengthy commitment. It's a great starting point if you're new to Claude Code or want to fill gaps in your existing knowledge. Pair it with an open VS Code window to follow along hands-on.

highlights.title: Claude Code and Codex Can Have Real-Time Conversations via Git
highlights.summary: This Medium article (surfaced on Hacker News) explores a creative technique where Claude Code and OpenAI Codex communicate with each other in real time by reading and writing to a shared Git repository. This opens up interesting multi-agent workflows where different AI coding assistants can collaborate asynchronously on the same codebase. It's an advanced but fascinating pattern for developers experimenting with agentic AI pipelines.

highlights.title: Show HN: Boxes.dev — Run Claude Code Agents in the Cloud
highlights.summary: Boxes.dev is a new cloud-only agentic development environment that gives each Claude Code (or Codex) agent its own isolated cloud computer, eliminating the need to run agents on your local machine. This is useful for developers who want to run long-running or parallel Claude Code tasks without tying up their laptop or worrying about local resource limits. It's worth exploring if you're scaling up agentic workflows beyond single-session use.

highlights.title: Show HN: Headroom — Stretch Your Claude Plan with Prompt Compression
highlights.summary: Headroom is a macOS menu bar app that silently trims prompt bloat, strips boilerplate, and compresses documents before they reach Claude Code, reportedly doubling your effective usage on existing Claude subscription plans. It works passively in the background without changing your workflow, making it a low-friction way to get more out of your current plan. A great tool for developers who frequently hit context or usage limits.

tips_of_the_day: Before letting Claude Code modify shell startup files like `.zshrc` or `.bash_login`, review the proposed changes carefully — these files run on every shell launch, so unintended edits can cause persistent side effects. Since v2.1.160, Claude Code will prompt you first, but it's still good practice to read the diff before accepting.

If your team uses Claude Code in a shared or enterprise environment, use the new `requiredMinimumVersion` managed setting (v2.1.163+) to pin all developers to a tested, approved version range — this prevents one person's accidental upgrade from breaking a shared workflow.

To get more mileage from your Claude subscription, experiment with keeping your prompts and context files lean: remove commented-out code, redundant README sections, and large auto-generated files from the context before starting a session. Smaller, focused context means faster responses and more tasks per billing period.
